what_it_does: Drafts a decision record in merge-SA decision-row shape for whether the company should adopt one shared substrate-home address/relation spine across its pieces while letting each piece keep its own engine/store, leaving the draft for the lead to curate before it becomes a rendered decision card.
observations: Recommended option is 'One shared spine — pieces keep their own engines'
The meaning explicitly states existing content on the shared backend is non-operational and should be read for intent then superseded, not migrated
The decision is bound to five held substrate decisions that name the shared address model
It includes a 'NOTES FOR THE LEAD' section asking about wording and render order
It uses a structured decision row with meaning, options (label/implication/recommended), scope, and state
standouts: The recommended unification is at the address/relation spine only, not a merge of engines or architectures
It explicitly reframes existing co-located backend content as a sketch to read-and-supersede rather than preserve
It applies the same merge-SA decision template to a foundation-kind decision
conventions: Follows a merge-SA decision-row shape: meaning, options array with label/implication/recommended, scope, state
Uses a decision://global/substrate-home style URI for the decision id
Frames options neutrally with implications rather than advocacy
Includes curation notes for the lead separate from the decision content
References related foundation decisions as prerequisites
concerns: The file is explicitly a DRAFT awaiting lead curation, so the final wording and render order are unresolved
It depends on five other substrate decisions that are not listed in this file
Option 3 proposes a sequencing bet without criteria or trigger for when convergence would follow
The meaning references external artifacts (convergence-map, as-is per part, prior discussions) not shown here
notes: A draft foundation decision asking whether to adopt one shared address/relation spine across company pieces while keeping their engines separate, with curation notes for the lead.
questions: What are the exact labels and contents of the five bound substrate decisions?
What is the full structure of the merge-SA/factory-shape decision template?
Who is Tim and what does 'master-scope #75' refer to?
What is the 'SUBSTRATE-HOME-CONVERGENCE-MAP.md' and the 'as-is per part' it draws on?
What is the intended render order: this decision first as a frame, or the five foundation decisions first?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is a drafted merge-SA decision row for the lead to curate, and that is exactly what it contains.
apparent_themes: Unify addressing and relations, not engines
Read-and-supersede rather than migrate legacy sketches
Foundation decisions must name the address model before this decision resolves
Decision records as curatable, renderable design substrates
Avoid accidental co-location being mistaken for unification
intention_signals: It is trying to capture a strategic architectural choice about substrate convergence, preserve two specific refinements from Tim, and produce a curatable decision card that can link to a set of identity/taxonomy foundation decisions.
novelty: It treats a foundation-level decision with the same merge-SA template as other decisions, parameterized by kind, and explicitly distinguishes spine-level address unification from engine consolidation.
contribution: A structured decision draft that separates the question of a shared address model from the preservation of per-piece engines, and that links the decision to a set of prerequisite foundation identity/taxonomy decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A draft merge-SA decision record for adopting a single shared substrate-home address and relation spine across company pieces while each piece retains its own storage engine.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a curatable foundation decision that captures Tim's refinements and frames the convergence question before rendering as a decision card.
